commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(security): move Anthropic API key server-side via Vercel function

api/parse-receipt.ts — new Vercel serverless function: receives base64
images, calls Anthropic with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (server-side secret),
returns ParseReceiptResult JSON.

src/ai/parseReceipt.ts — remove @anthropic-ai/sdk import, POST to
/api/parse-receipt instead. API key never reaches the browser bundle.

Tests updated to mock fetch instead of @anthropic-ai/sdk — all 9 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: validate_grow_output and POLISH deterministic read beat_ids not node_ids on intersection_group (#1172)

## Summary

Corrects a field name mismatch: two consumers of `intersection_group`
nodes were reading `node_ids` instead of the canonical `beat_ids` field,
causing intersection group validation to silently fail and intersection
passages to not be formed.

**Files changed:**
- `src/questfoundry/graph/polish_validation.py` line 219:
`group_data.get("node_ids", [])` → `group_data.get("beat_ids", [])`
- `src/questfoundry/pipeline/stages/polish/deterministic.py` line 262:
`node_ids = group_data.get("node_ids", [])` → `raw_beat_ids =
group_data.get("beat_ids", [])`

## Design Conformance

This is a targeted field-name bug fix against the spec-defined
`intersection_group` schema (Doc 3 §7). The architect-reviewer was
consulted on the broader investigation that uncovered this bug (#1172
GitHub issue).

| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| `intersection_group` nodes store beat membership as `beat_ids` (Doc 3
§7) | CONFORMANT — both consumers now read `beat_ids` |
| `_check_beat_grouping` validates that all beats in an intersection
group are grouped | CONFORMANT |
| POLISH Phase 4 uses `beat_ids` to form intersection passages |
CONFORMANT |

MISSING/DEAD findings: **0**

## Test Plan

- [x] Updated `test_polish_deterministic.py`: renamed existing test to
`test_intersection_group_uses_beat_ids` and added
`test_intersection_group_node_ids_field_not_used` regression test
- [x] Updated `test_polish_entry_contract.py`: fixture now uses
`beat_ids` (spec-conformant)
- All CI checks pass

Closes #1172

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: questfoundry
repository.description: Pipeline-driven interactive fiction generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

i9: Phase 4 — ACH matrices, Devil's Advocacy, Dung semantics

Complete the four-phase architectural roadmap. ACH builds
hypothesis × evidence consistency matrices with weighted scoring.
Devil's Advocacy generates deterministic adversarial challenges
(source authority, single source, overconfidence, circular
corroboration). Dung argumentation computes grounded extension
(iterative fixpoint) and preferred extensions (maximal admissible
sets). All 6 adjudicator skills now real. 103 tests, all green.

commit.message:
SSH module: regenerate .pub key if missing

If only the private key exists (e.g. restored from 1Password or copied
manually), regenerate the public key with ssh-keygen -y. This prevents
git SSH signing from failing when commit.gpgsign=true points at a
non-existent .pub file.

commit.message:
Use IPFS source images for evaders instead of Alchemy CDN

Alchemy CDN strips backgrounds from grayscale evader PNGs, causing
checkerboard transparency. Now fetchEvaderById and Boneyard prefer
the IPFS originalUrl which has proper backgrounds baked in. Reverted
all CSS background hacks — no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move directory field out of repository block in goreleaser config

GoReleaser v2 expects `directory` at the brew level, not nested under
`repository`. This was causing release builds to fail for v0.5.0+.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand §1.6 with derivations and worked examples; fix inverse formula bug

Bug fixed: i_1 = i_CM + i_12 was wrong; correct formula is
i_1 = i_CM/2 + i_12 (total CM is shared equally among N lines).

New content in §1.6:
- Derive current inverse (N=2): show algebra step-by-step from
  the two forward equations, leading to boxed results
- Derive voltage inverse (N=2): same approach for v_1P, v_2P
- Worked current example: i_12=8A, i_CM=0.4A → i_1=8.2A, i_2=-7.8A;
  full verify step; physbox explaining the 0.4A imbalance as EMI
- Worked voltage example: v_12=600V, v_CM=300V → v_1P=600V, v_2P=0V;
  full verify step; physbox explaining why v_2P=0
- Warningbox: factor-of-N pitfall and how to spot the error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
kind-pasteur-2026-03-06-S25g: w0=H-3t3 verified n=5, P(n)=A093934 rooted tournaments

- w_0 = H - 3*t_3 exhaustively verified for all 1024 n=5 tournaments
- Formula FAILS at n=7 (needs invariants beyond t_3, t_5)
- Perspective counting P(n) = 2*(n-1)! verified n<=5, FAILS at n=6 (296 != 240)
- P(n) matches OEIS A093934 = rooted tournament iso classes
- Signed backward-parity count W(0) = (1/2)^{n-1} * (E-O) verified

commit.message:
refactor: split 11 large files into smaller focused modules (Phase 4) (#488)

* refactor: split 11 large files into smaller focused modules (Phase 4)

Split files exceeding 400 lines into smaller, focused modules:

API Routes:
- agent-search/route.ts (1724→333) → schemas, sse-helpers, request-handlers, batch/streaming handlers
- articles/list/route.ts (1006→398) → query-helpers, response-builder, cache-config, types
- graph-data-serializer.ts (960→332) → node-transformer, link-builder, graph-selection, depth-serializer

Backend Services:
- trend-report-generator.ts (1205→291) → trend-report/ subdir with types, data-aggregator, ai-summary-generator, prompts, validators
- summary-manager.ts (714→279) → summary/ subdir with batch-processor, orchestrator, types
- category-filter-service.ts (749→431) → filters/ subdir with candidate-extractor, score-aggregator

Utils:
- summary-quality-checker.ts (706→364) → quality-rules, quality-scorer, quality-types
- content-quality-checker.ts (617→316) → quality-rules

Frontend:
- DailyTrendHero.tsx (738→348) → KeyTopicArticleCards, StructuredAISummaryView
- filters.tsx (695→64) → useSourceFilter hook, SourceFilterPanel
- agent-answer-panel.tsx (661→190) → answer-content, source-references

Re-export files maintain backward compatibility for all existing importers.
No circular dependencies in split areas (verified with madge).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/fix prisma performance jivm q (#18)

* Fix request hanging: add timeout to readiness gate + trending error handling

- Readiness middleware now has 3s timeout and caches success, preventing
  /api/me and all other routes from hanging while ALTER TABLE runs
- Added full error handling to /posts/trending with schema drift fallback
  and minimal query fallback, returns [] instead of 500 on failure

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AcwMhR3VG3XMPATgKDH7b2

* Add ultra-minimal raw SQL fallback for feed when DB columns are missing

The raw emergency query was referencing columns (tokenName, mcap1h, isWin1h,
percentChange1h, viewCount, dexscreenerUrl, isVerified) that may not exist
in the database. Now loadEmergencyFeedPostsRaw tries the full query first,
and if it fails, falls back to a minimal query using only core columns
(id, content, authorId, contractAddress, entryMcap, settled, isWin, etc.)
that are guaranteed to exist in any schema version.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AcwMhR3VG3XMPATgKDH7b2

* Pin prisma@6 in all scripts + add minimal raw SQL fallback for feed

- Pin all prisma CLI commands to prisma@6 to prevent bunx/npx resolving
  to Prisma 7 which has breaking schema changes (no url in datasource)
- Switch from bunx to npx for more reliable local package resolution
- Add ultra-minimal raw SQL fallback that only queries core columns
  (id, content, authorId, contractAddress, entryMcap, settled, isWin)
  guaranteed to exist in any DB version

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AcwMhR3VG3XMPATgKDH7b2

Fix multi-word search returning 0 results: disable phrase verification for unquoted queries

Unquoted multi-word queries like "serpent seed" were triggering exact phrase
verification, which filtered out sermons where the words appear separately
(e.g. "seed of the serpent"). Now only quoted phrases get exact-match
verification; unquoted multi-word queries use AND intersection only.

commit.message:
fix: JavaScript syntax error preventing dashboard from loading

Add [] for computed property name in downloadExcel() object literal.
`monthCap + ' Incentive'` → `[monthCap + ' Incentive']`

This syntax error caused the ENTIRE inline script to fail parsing,
preventing DOMContentLoaded handler from registering, which left
the loading overlay stuck indefinitely.
